checklist.note: All 6 review blocks state a REAL invariant that holds at the anchored source at HEAD. Verified against code: events.py:74 Event is @dataclass(frozen=True) (sentinel L73); transitions.py:29 _TRANSITIONS dict is the sole matrix consulted by compute_target_state (sentinel L28); identity.py:45 resolve_identity implements override>env(SUPER_HARNESS_ACTOR)>git user.email>'cli', first non-empty after strip (sentinel L44); gates/decisions.py:20 PRE_TOOL_USE_DECISIONS single literal, docstring+__all__ confirm daemon+in-process both read it (sentinel L19); decision_check.py run_check routes dangling_up into ok=False (block) and dangling_down separate (warn) (sentinel L9); decisions.py _VALID_STATUSES = 4 states + _ID_RE one-file-per-record (sentinel L7). Each block carries the reconcile/betray protocol. All 9 decisions text-locked (ratified_text_hash present). 2:7 hard:context tier split (2 pre-existing tier-1).

git diff main...HEAD touches exactly 6 files, all under docs/decisions/ (+104/-6). No src/ changed — confirmed the @decision: sentinels already existed in source (diff stat shows zero source edits). Stayed within the stated 6-file scope.

Frontmatter additions are well-formed: re-ratify text-lock (ratified_text_hash), reconciled baseline (reconciled_anchors with byte-sha256), self reconcile metadata. Spot-checked reconciled_anchors hashes for events.py and identity.py — both MATCH current source bytes, so the 'invariant holds at HEAD' baseline is genuine not stale. decision check exits 0 / 'clean', so no integrity_violations, no unhashed_ratified, no unreconciled_tier2.

Anti-hollow audit: none of the 6 is a tautology — each describes something a future change could plausibly betray (reorder identity precedence, fork gate policy into a second table, mutate/truncate an event, flip up/down dangling semantics, add a 5th state or multi-record file, route a state change outside _TRANSITIONS) and a human can check by reading the named file. No-tier-1 call is honest for all 6: none has a non-hollow single-token grep that captures the full invariant — the real invariants are ordering/scope/semantic (e.g. identity precedence is multi-line ordering; 'no ad-hoc transition elsewhere' and 'both readers defer to one SSOT' are whole-codebase semantics). A grep for frozen=True or _TRANSITIONS would assert existence but not the invariant, i.e. hollow. Same honest tier-2 mechanism as the existing d-state-pure-fold. Minor inherent limitation (not a defect): d-events-append-only and d-dangling-check assert behavior that partly lives outside the anchored file (the event writer / the CLI exit-2 layer), so the byte-anchor only re-routes review when the anchored file changes; consistent with how byte-anchoring works project-wide and the invariant statements remain correct.

Change IS documentation (decision records). No source, schema, or AGENTS.md impact. Arming follows the AGENTS.md recipe: tier-2 reviewable where no brittle one-token signature exists, no invented hollow tier-1 check. Removes the prior 'ratified before text-lock' standing warning by re-ratifying.
